## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-8-9df09ce7`

finding_summary: The new workstream-order reporting can overstate which workstreams are actually interleaved. `analyze_workstream_order()` treats every workstream left with non-zero indegree after Kahn reduction as `blocked` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:855-859`), and `render_plan_md_checked()` prints that whole set as the interleaving group (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1568-1577`). In a valid bead DAG where only workstreams A and B interleave but a third workstream merely depends on the end of B, `plan.md` will incorrectly claim that third workstream is part of the interleaving set.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs"]
remediation_hint: Compute the actual cycle/SCC participants before rendering the "interleave across ..." list, instead of using every node that remains blocked after topological reduction.
